Author: polyfractal
Content: I should start working on my new designs in earnest I guess! A lot sooner than I was thinking for some reason...probably best not to repeat the frantic finish-in-one-month sprint like the first run 😅

Content: I have a spreadsheet @ https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1KD2Mn7s7WoZ738tmH0LNp_uvDCJVLOdqiyMbqEh0ApU/edit?gid=0#gid=0 - but expect dates to change as I have yet to get confirmation for the wafer out date from GF.

Author: mole99
Content: That's a good resolution, but it never works out. In practice, you always end up making last-minute changes 😆

Author: 246tnt
Content: Did GF confirm fab out now ?

Author: mithro_
Content: I /think/ I got the wafers out late date yesterday but I won't be able to confirm until Monday that is what the date provided actually is.

Author: thorben_61995
Content: In Run 2, will there be an opportunity to purchase larger slots than 1x1, like 2x1 or 2x2? Or would it mess up all the dicing/sorting?

Author: mithro_
Content: They do mess up the dicing / sorting. I have been focusing on smaller slots. Do you actually have the funds for bigger slots?

Author: thorben_61995
Content: That's a pity. Yes, we have funds for bigger prototypes and the 1x1 slot could not fit everything that I wanted. But of course I understand that it makes limited sense to accomodate larger slots if only one person is interested... Anyone else in need of more area in future runs?

Author: mithro_
Content: What was your limit? There is plenty of work to improve things like typical place and route density and such

Author: thorben_61995
Content: I already had a utilization of 65% with >400k standard cells (w/o the macros) and it fit about half the logic I wanted, so PnR improvements won't get me there. Proprietary synthesis would probably compress the logic quite a bit, but working with proprietary tools is not an option for this project. Except for larger slots I currently don't really see a solution. If it helps, I think we would be able to pay a reasonable premium for a larger slot and would not necessarily need as many samples.

Author: 246tnt
Content:
Options I can think off the top of my head are :
* Switch to laser dicing
* Run more wafers and dice those just to separate those particular dies
* Switch an entire row / col to the larger format

Author: mole99
Content: Laser dicing would give us a lot of flexibility in mixing different slot sizes.

Author: 246tnt
Content: Who's doing the dicing ATM ? Is it GF themselves ?

Author: mithro_
Content: So far I have not found a cost effective laser dicing place.
